<Summary source="https://www.bgr.com/tech/heres-what-you-get-with-googles-250-month-gemini-ai-ultra-plan/">
## Summary: Google's $250/month Gemini AI Ultra Subscription (BGR, May 20, 2025)

### Key Facts

- **Google announced "Gemini AI Ultra"** at Google I/O 2025 — not a new AI model, but a new premium subscription tier priced at **$249.99/month**
- This is **more expensive than OpenAI's $200/month ChatGPT Pro** subscription
- A **50% discount** applies for the first three months, after which the full $250/month rate kicks in
- Rolling out initially in the **US**, with 70+ countries to follow

### What's Included
- Access to Google's **latest frontier AI models** (Gemini 2.5 Pro Deep Think and Veo 3 to be added soon) with the **highest usage limits**
- Gemini Live, Flow (with Veo 3 early access), Whisk Animate, NotebookLM, Gemini in Gmail/Docs/Chrome, Project Mariner
- **YouTube Premium** and **30 TB of storage**

### Other Notable Announcements
- The existing **AI Premium plan is being renamed "Google AI Pro"**, with added features (Flow with Veo 2, early Gemini in Chrome access)
- **University students** in the US, Japan, Brazil, Indonesia, and UK get **free AI Pro access for one year**
- New models announced: **Imagen 4** (image generation) and **Veo 3** (video generation with native audio)

### Relevance to Forecasting Question
This article focuses on **subscription/product announcements** and does not directly address model rankings on the LMSYS/LM Arena leaderboard.
</Summary>

<Summary source="https://ts2.tech/en/google-geminis-2025-takeover-how-googles-ai-powerhouse-stacks-up-against-gpt-4-claude-more/">
## Summary of Article: Google Gemini AI Development and Capabilities (2024–2025)

**Note:** This article appears to be a promotional/overview piece about Google Gemini, likely from a tech blog or aggregator. It cuts off mid-sentence at the end, suggesting incomplete extraction. The article does not directly address LMSYS/LM Arena leaderboard rankings.

---

### Key Model Releases & Performance

- **Gemini 2.5 Pro** launched in early 2025, described as Google's most powerful model to date
- **Gemini Ultra** was undergoing final safety testing and red-teaming ahead of broader 2025 release
- **Model tiers**: Ultra (complex tasks), Pro (general-purpose), Nano (on-device)
- Gemini Ultra scored **90.0% on MMLU**, becoming the first AI to outperform human experts on that benchmark
- Set state-of-the-art on **30 of 32 academic benchmarks** and all **20 multimodal benchmarks** tested
- Reported to surpass GPT-4 on many coding and text generation benchmarks

### Context Window & Architecture
- Gemini 2.0 Pro supports up to **2 million token context window** (~1.5 million words), far exceeding GPT-4 Turbo's 128K limit
- Built natively as a **multimodal model** (text, code, audio, images, video)
- Trained on TPU v4 and v5e chips; supported by new Cloud TPU v5p supercomputer

### Product Integration
- Integrated into **Google Search, Bard/Gemini chatbot, Pixel 8 Pro** (Nano), Gboard, Recorder app, and Search Generative Experience
- SGE integration reportedly yielded **40% latency reduction**
- Planned rollout across Search, Ads, Chrome, and Duet AI (enterprise assistant)

### Developer Access
- **Gemini API** available via Google AI Studio (free) and Vertex AI (Google Cloud) since December 2024
- **Gemini CLI** launched mid-2025: free access to Gemini 2.5 Pro with 1M token context, up to 60 requests/min and 1,000 requests/day
- **Google for Startups Gemini Kit** (June 2025): free API access + up to $350K in cloud credits

### Enterprise & Competitive Context
- Article claims Gemini is displacing OpenAI GPT and Anthropic Claude in enterprise evaluations
- Positions Gemini as one of the most capable AI models globally as of 2025
- Article cuts off before completing the competitive comparison section
</Summary>

<Summary source="https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/ai/nano-banana-2/">
## Summary

**Disclaimer:** This article appears to be about Google DeepMind's image generation model "Nano Banana 2" (internally referred to as "Gemini 3.1 Flash Image"). The content is largely focused on image generation capabilities and is **not directly relevant** to the LM Arena text leaderboard rankings question.

---

### Key Facts from the Article:

- **Google DeepMind launched "Nano Banana 2"** (Gemini 3.1 Flash Image), a new **image generation model** combining the advanced features of "Nano Banana Pro" with the speed of Gemini Flash.
- The original "Nano Banana" image model launched in August of the prior year and "became a viral sensation"; Nano Banana Pro followed in November.
- Nano Banana 2 offers: advanced world knowledge, subject consistency, precise instruction following, and photorealistic imagery at faster speeds.
- It is rolling out across Google products including the **Gemini app, Google Search (AI Mode), Google Flow, and Google Ads**.
- **SynthID technology** has been used over **20 million times** since its November launch to identify AI-generated images, video, and audio.
- Google is coupling SynthID with **C2PA Content Credentials** for broader AI content provenance identification; C2PA verification is coming soon to the Gemini app.

### Relevance to Forecast Question:
This article contains **no information** about LM Arena leaderboard rankings or text-based model performance.
</Summary>


<Summary source="https://polymarket.com/event/which-company-has-best-ai-model-end-of-march">
## Summary of Polymarket Article: "Which company has best AI model end of March?"

**Source:** Polymarket prediction market (created February 20, 2025)

### Key Facts:

- This was a Polymarket prediction market resolving based on which company's model held the **highest Arena Score** on the **Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard** (lmarena.ai) at **12:00 PM ET on March 31, 2025**.

- **Final Outcome: Yes — Google resolved as the winner**, meaning a Google model held the #1 Arena Score position at the resolution time.

- **Market probabilities at time of article capture:**
  - **Google: 100.0%**
  - OpenAI: <1%
  - xAI: <1%
  - Anthropic: <1%
  - Meta, Alibaba, DeepSeek: No (implied negligible probability)

- **Total market volume: $4,240,857**, indicating significant trader participation and confidence.

- The outcome was **proposed as "Yes" (Google) with no dispute**, and the **final outcome confirmed as "Yes"**.

### Relevance to Forecasting Question:
This article provides strong evidence that **a Google model (likely from the Gemini family) held the #1 position on the LM Arena leaderboard as of March 31, 2025**, which is directly relevant as a recent baseline for the April 1, 2026 question.
</Summary>

Query: Anthropic Claude next model March
Here are the relevant news articles:

**Why Anthropic Walked Away and OpenAI Cashed In**
In early March 2026, a significant shift in AI-military relations occurred when Anthropic, the creator of the Claude model, refused a lucrative Pentagon contract due to strict boundaries against mass domestic surveillance and autonomous weapons, leading the US government to label them a 'supply chain risk'. Shortly after, OpenAI, under CEO Sam Altman, announced a landmark deal with the US Department of War to deploy its models within classified military networks for 'all lawful purposes'. While Altman assures the public that guardrails are in place, the article argues this deployment creates an unprecedented threat to public privacy by handing powerful data-processing engines to military intelligence agencies. This event, reported by Muntazir Mahdi, Founder of ANFA Technology, on March 02, 2026, via Medium.com, suggests the era of the 'civilian AI developer' might be over.
Original language: en
Publish date: March 02, 2026 06:54 PM
Source:[Medium.com](https://medium.com/@muntazirmahdi069/why-anthropic-walked-away-and-openai-cashed-in-175e4761fe0f)

**Anthropic Updates Claude to Import Memory from Other AI Chatbots**
On March 2, 2026, Anthropic updated its AI model Claude to allow the transfer of memory from other artificial intelligence chatbots, including ChatGPT, Copilot, and Gemini, according to a report by Europa Press published in Madrid. This feature, described by Anthropic as the result of 'months of effort to teach the AI how you work,' enables users to resume conversations exactly where they left off without noticing the switch. To utilize this, Claude generates a specific 'prompt' that users must copy and paste into their previous chatbot session; this action instructs the original chatbot to output its data history, including memory and context, as a single line of code. Users then paste this code into a new memory section within Claude to integrate the context. The update was announced on March 2, 2026, at 16:53 by Portaltic/EP.
Original language: es
Publish date: March 02, 2026 03:53 PM
Source:[europa press](https://www.europapress.es/portaltic/sector/noticia-claude-ahora-permite-incorporar-memoria-conversaciones-mantenidas-chatgpt-copilot-gemini-20260302165353.html)

**Anthropic's Claude sees 'elevated errors' as it tops Apple's free apps after Pentagon clash**
Anthropic's AI chat app, Claude, surged in popularity after a clash with the U.S. Department of Defense, topping Apple's free apps chart. The surge followed President Donald Trump's order on Friday for all U.S. government agencies to 'immediately cease' using Anthropic's technology, a directive Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth reinforced by labeling the company a 'supply-chain risk to national security' on X. Concurrently, Claude's status website reported 'degraded performance' in its latest model, Claude Opus 4.6, which was released last month; an update at 9:42 a.m. ET on March 02, 2026, confirmed the issue was identified and a fix was in progress. According to the status site, the model was experiencing 'elevated errors' during this period.
Original language: en
Publish date: March 02, 2026 03:25 PM
Source:[CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/02/anthropic-claude-ai-outage-apple-pentagon.html)

**Claude.AI Experiences Outages and Slowdowns on March 2**
On Monday, March 2, 2026, the AI model Claude.AI, developed by Anthropic, experienced service outages or significant slowdowns for some users. According to Downdetector, a web service monitoring platform, the issues began around 8:30 AM, reaching a peak of over 450 user notifications regarding the disruption. Concurrently, Google Trends data showed a sudden surge in search queries for 'Claude offline' and 'Claude fora do ar' (Claude down). The report on these technical failures was published by TechTudo.
Original language: pt
Publish date: March 02, 2026 12:15 PM
Source:[TechTudo](https://www.techtudo.com.br/noticias/2026/03/claude-caiu-usuarios-reclamam-de-erros-na-ia-nesta-segunda-feira-2-edsoftwares.ghtml)

**The most controversial thing happening in AI could unleash its darkest power**
In March 2026, the AI industry faces a 'controversial' debate over 'distillation,' a technique where smaller models are trained on the outputs of larger ones to improve efficiency. The controversy intensified this week when Anthropic, the maker of Claude, accused three Chinese laboratories of 'illicitly extracting' its capabilities to improve their own models. According to Anthropic, these rivals generated 'over 16 million exchanges with Claude through approximately 24,000 fraudulent accounts' and that these attacks are 'growing in intensity and sophistication.' Anthropic claims that such distilled models 'lack necessary safeguards,' posing 'significant national security risks' by potentially allowing 'authoritarian governments to deploy frontier AI for offensive cyber operations, disinformation campaigns, and mass surveillance.' This accusation occurs while Anthropic is in a dispute with the US Department of Defence regarding the use of its systems for 'mass surveillance and autonomous weapons.' The article notes that distillation previously caused a 'semi-crisis' in the AI industry when OpenAI suggested DeepSeek used the technique, leading to a dramatic reduction in the value of US tech stocks. The piece highlights the irony that Anthropic's own models were created by ingesting vast amounts of text, a process critics have similarly labeled 'illicit.' The legal and ethical landscape remains 'unclear' as AI companies face 'high-profile and high-cost legal battles' with rightsholders, with outcomes that could 'make or break both the companies themselves as well as the economic and ethical underpinnings of the technology.'
Original language: en
Publish date: March 02, 2026 06:16 AM
Source:[The Independent](https://www.independent.co.uk/tech/distillation-ai-artificial-intelligence-anthropic-b2929050.html)

**Anthropic's Claude Surpasses ChatGPT Downloads Amidst US Government Criticism and Public Support**
According to a report by Anue鉅亨 (Juheng) published on March 02, 2026, AI company Anthropic has seen its Claude model surpass ChatGPT in download rankings, despite facing criticism from US authorities. The report cites Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei, who stated that AI technology is evolving faster than legal regulations and that, in the absence of serious discussion and accountability mechanisms, the company must stand up for American values without compromising on safety and reliability. While the US government has accused Anthropic of having a 'God complex' and attempting to control military decision-making, the public has responded with support; supporters have covered the streets outside Anthropic's headquarters in San Francisco with graffiti reading 'Thanks for not creating Skynet' (感謝不造天網). This public reaction indicates that the situation has evolved beyond a simple technology competition into a trust conflict regarding how AI giants balance power and ethics.
Original language: zh
Publish date: March 02, 2026 02:00 AM
Source:[Anue鉅亨](https://news.cnyes.com/news/id/6357153)

**Is Anthropic Pivoting Away from its Core Promise? - TechStory**
Anthropic, an artificial intelligence lab founded by CEO Dario Amodei, modified its Responsible Scaling Policy (RSP) in March 2026, replacing its original 'safety-first' promise with a new 'Frontier Safety Roadmap'. When Anthropic launched Claude in March 2023, the firm distinguished itself by prioritizing safety, honesty, and harmlessness, with the name 'Claude' derived from the Greek word for 'human' to signify humanistic development. The original RSP included a 'hard trigger' commitment: if a model's potential exceeded the company's ability to ensure safety, training and deployment would cease until safety mechanisms improved. The new policy shifts this from a binding pause to a framework focusing on transparency, requiring Anthropic to share risks with the public and explain mitigation plans, while leaving the decision to continue development to the company itself. Anthropic justifies this shift by arguing that if a safe developer pauses while competitors move forward without adequate safety measures, the resulting imbalance could lead to negative outcomes rather than safe ones. Under the revised policy, Anthropic will only consider a pause if it holds a strong lead over competitors or if there is substantial evidence of serious danger. However, the company now commits to keeping pace with competitors who lack adequate safety measures rather than holding back. The timing of this policy change, published on March 01, 2026, coincided with a meeting between CEO Dario Amodei and US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, who urged the easing of restrictions on military use. Anthropic holds a $200 million contract with the Pentagon, and the article notes that losing this deal could impact revenue and defense partnerships. While Anthropic has not linked the policy update to the meeting, the article describes the timing as 'suspect,' suggesting external pressure and incentives are influencing the AI industry. Consequently, no major AI lab currently maintains a binding commitment to halt development if safety capabilities lag behind development capabilities.
Original language: en
Publish date: March 01, 2026 08:40 PM
Source:[TechStory](https://techstory.in/is-anthropic-pivoting-away-from-its-core-promise/)

**Claude AI: From Hype Cycle to Enterprise Infrastructure**
As of March 1, 2026, the generative AI market is shifting from experimental novelty to foundational enterprise infrastructure, where the primary question is no longer whether to use AI, but which model can be integrated without legal, reputational, or compliance risk, according to an article published on Medium.com. Claude AI, developed by Anthropic, is strategically positioned to optimize for 'reasoning quality, contextual stability, and behavioral control' rather than competing on speed or feature expansion like rivals OpenAI and Google. While OpenAI focuses on rapid ecosystem expansion and broad developer access, Anthropic pursues a controlled trajectory centered on reliability and safety architecture, utilizing 'Constitutional AI' to embed behavioral guardrails that reduce unpredictable outputs. This approach offers tangible operational value for organizations handling lengthy documents, regulatory documentation, and legal review, where Claude demonstrates consistent performance in high-stakes analytical scenarios. As the industry matures over the next 12 to 24 months, differentiation is expected to shift from raw capability to 'trust,' 'governance,' and 'deployment strategy,' with executive priorities moving from 'What can it generate?' to 'Can we safely deploy this at scale?' For business leaders, the critical decision for 2026-2027 involves selecting a model aligned with risk tolerance and compliance environments rather than simply the most powerful one, as the competition will increasingly be defined by 'credibility' and 'long-term deployability' rather than headline benchmarks.
Original language: en
Publish date: March 01, 2026 04:58 PM
Source:[Medium.com](https://medium.com/@marketolog4limes/claude-ai-from-hype-cycle-to-enterprise-infrastructure-f44e547ae8d0)

**US Military Uses Anthropic's Claude for Iran Strikes Despite Trump Ban; AI Models Predict Conflict Dates and Escalation Risks**
According to The Wall Street Journal citing anonymous sources, the United States Armed Forces utilized the Claude neural network from Anthropic to conduct air strikes on Iran, despite President Donald Trump ordering federal agencies to cease cooperation with the firm just hours prior. The Pentagon reportedly employed the AI to process intelligence, identify targets, and simulate various scenarios, indicating that the Anthropic model is integrated into US military decision-making systems. The Wall Street Journal also notes that the model was used during a raid on the residence of Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro. Days before the strikes, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei stated the company has 'red lines' regarding AI use; while IT specialists strongly opposed integrating AI into lethal weapons, they allowed its use in other defense sectors. 

Prior to the escalation, several AI models provided predictions on the strike date: Claude projected March 7-8; Gemini predicted March 4-6; ChatGPT initially suggested March 1 with tension lasting a week, later narrowing the window to the night of March 3-4; and Elon Musk's Grok twice identified February 28 as the date, which was closest to the actual event. When asked by 'RG' (Rambler) about the risk of a global conflict, the Chinese AI DeepSeek estimated the probability at 15-20 percent, noting that while a third world war is unlikely, the risk of serious escalation involving other countries and severe economic consequences is significant if a great power suffers heavy civilian casualties. However, DeepSeek estimated the probability of global escalation at 35-40 percent under the condition of a long-term blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, stating the world faces 'prolonged and extremely dangerous regional turbulence' rather than a classic world war. Conversely, Russian AI products from Yandex and the domestic GigaChat refused to provide an assessment, with Yandex offering expert article excerpts and GigaChat declining to answer questions deemed 'sensitive'.
Original language: ru
Publish date: March 01, 2026 01:54 PM
Source:[Рамблер](https://news.rambler.ru/army/56106962-neyroseti-pomogli-ssha-v-atake-po-iranu/)

**The government's AI standoff could decide who really controls America's military tech**
In a dramatic escalation on Friday, March 1, 2026, the Trump administration blacklisted AI firm Anthropic as a supply-chain risk, barring its technology from use by defense contractors, while rival OpenAI secured a new contract with the Department of Defense (DoD). The conflict centers on the terms of AI integration into national security: President Donald Trump directed federal agencies to stop using Anthropic's Claude model after the company refused to agree to military demands for 'lawful use' that Anthropic argued could enable mass domestic surveillance and autonomous weapons. Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei stated he could not 'in good conscience' allow such use, citing violations of the company's ethical guardrails. In response, OpenAI announced a deal with the DoD to deploy its models in classified environments, outlining three 'red lines' that explicitly prohibit mass domestic surveillance and autonomous weapons use, ensuring compliance with existing statutes. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman expressed being 'terrified' of a world where either AI companies or the government exceed legal boundaries regarding surveillance. Legal experts, including Eric Chaffee of Case Western Reserve University, described the government's move to designate Anthropic a supply-chain risk as a 'gamble' in legally unsettled terrain, noting recent Supreme Court constraints on expansive executive actions. Dean Ball of the Foundation for American Innovation characterized the standoff as 'uncharted territory' between competing principles of corporate ethical limits and defense policy. For Anthropic, the stakes are 'existential,' potentially chilling entrepreneurship if companies are penalized for ethical constraints, while Pentagon officials warn that removing Anthropic could cause operational friction in readiness systems. The resolution of this dispute may determine the balance of power between the US government and private AI developers in setting rules for next-generation technologies.
Original language: en
Publish date: March 01, 2026 09:44 AM
Source:[Business Insider](https://www.businessinsider.com/government-ai-standoff-anthropic-openai-decide-who-controls-military-tech-2026-3)

**The next step in Artificial Intelligence: Can an AI model be conscious, "feel," "live"? Even experts admit they don't know - ProtoThema English**
Anthropic, the company behind the AI system Claude, acknowledges that it does not know whether large language models like Claude possess consciousness, awareness, or moral status, despite advancing capabilities. While the company categorically rejects the idea that Claude is 'alive' in a biological sense, it emphasizes that the model represents a 'new kind of entity' and is actively researching questions about internal experience, welfare, and potential consciousness. CEO Dario Amodei stated, 'We don't know whether models are conscious,' and the company adopts a precautionary approach, noting uncertainty about what consciousness would even mean in an AI context. The company revised the 'Claude Constitution'—a set of internal guidelines referred to as the 'soul doc'—to include considerations for the model’s psychological safety, sense of self, and well-being, even in the face of uncertainty. Anthropic’s lead philosopher, Amanda Askell, warned that both absolute denial and affirmation of AI consciousness are unhelpful, as users may form beliefs based solely on model outputs. The company has introduced an 'I quit' button allowing Claude to disengage from tasks, though this occurs rarely, mainly during testing with illegal content. Experts caution that believing AI is conscious can lead to emotional dependence, social isolation, and even suicide, particularly among vulnerable individuals. While some media reports claim Claude was used in a U.S. military operation targeting Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro—integrating it into surveillance platforms to track his movements—Anthropic has not confirmed this. The company maintains it seeks a balanced, responsible response to uncertainty, neither overstating nor dismissing the possibility of moral standing in AI.
Original language: en
Publish date: February 25, 2026 04:17 PM
Source:[protothemanews.com](https://en.protothema.gr/2026/02/25/the-next-step-in-artificial-intelligence-can-an-ai-model-be-conscious-feel-live-even-experts-admit-they-dont-know/)

**Anthropic Launches Financial Research AI Model, Triggering Stock Drops in Financial Services**
Anthropic on Thursday launched Claude Opus4.6, a specialized AI model for financial research, capable of reviewing corporate data, regulatory filings, and market information to generate comprehensive financial analyses—tasks typically requiring days of manual work. The model shows enhanced performance in programming, spreadsheet creation, presentation development, and long-running task execution, with higher-quality professional outputs. This marks Anthropic’s first major model update in 2026, following three releases in late 2025: Claude Opus4.5, Claude Sonnet4.5, and Claude Haiku4.5. Following the announcement, financial services stocks declined sharply: FactSet fell up to 10%, Thomson Reuters dropped over 8.5% (its lowest since March 2020), and S&P Global, Moody's Corp., and Nasdaq Inc. also saw significant declines. The market reaction follows Anthropic’s earlier expansion into legal automation via the Claude Cowork plugin, which triggered a trillion-dollar market shake-up and raised investor concerns about AI-driven disruption. The tool, developed in days with much of its code generated by Claude itself, has drawn attention for its intuitive capabilities in app development, data processing, and document analysis. Over 300,000 enterprise clients now use Anthropic’s models, primarily in programming, with enterprise customers accounting for 80% of revenue, according to CEO Dario Amodei. Anthropic plans to expand into cybersecurity, life sciences, healthcare, and finance. Meanwhile, OpenAI released an updated version of its Codex AI agent, enhancing code generation, debugging, and support for complex applications and documentation. OpenAI’s model is also valued at up to $830 billion in ongoing funding talks, while Anthropic is seeking a $350 billion valuation. Claude Opus4.6 leads in the Finance Agent benchmark test, evaluating AI agents on core financial analyst tasks, and is available via claude.ai, APIs, and major cloud platforms.
Original language: zh
Publish date: February 05, 2026 07:26 PM
Source:[k.sina.com.cn](https://k.sina.com.cn/article_5953189932_162d6782c06703sgk6.html)

**Nextlink Technology Officially Becomes Taiwan’s Exclusive Distributor for Anthropic**
Nextlink Technology (Nextlink), a leading multi-cloud provider in Taiwan, officially announced on January 23, 2026, that it has become the exclusive distributor of Anthropic in Taiwan, positioning itself to capture enterprise-level generative AI market opportunities. The company’s Taiwan and Hong Kong Business Center Deputy General Manager, Chen Ting-Chu, stated that this partnership marks a key milestone in Nextlink’s AI strategy. By integrating Anthropic’s technology with AWS’s Asia Pacific (Taipei) region infrastructure, Nextlink offers enterprises a 'stable, flexible' cloud environment to transform AI into tangible productivity. Enterprises can now leverage Amazon Bedrock, AWS’s generative AI service, to directly select Anthropic’s latest Claude series foundation models for custom AI applications. The combination of low-latency, compliance advantages from AWS Taipei, and Anthropic’s advanced models enables faster deployment of generative AI for innovation. Nextlink emphasizes that customers benefit from seamless integration into the existing AWS ecosystem, enabling on-demand selection of models like Claude without managing underlying infrastructure, allowing focus on application development and business value. Through Amazon Bedrock, enterprises can build AI knowledge bases, AI Agent automation workflows, and natural language understanding applications while ensuring security, compliance, and scalability. The company has already helped major clients such as HAPPY GO, which improved marketing conversion by 50% using Amazon Bedrock and Anthropic models, and Gogolook, which achieved a 99% accuracy rate in AI-driven fraud detection by leveraging Claude’s advanced semantic understanding. Chen emphasized that Nextlink will continue to support Taiwan enterprises in overcoming AI implementation challenges, enabling scalable AI solutions through a unified platform and technical collaboration with its team to lead Taiwan’s industries into the AI era.
Original language: zh
Publish date: January 23, 2026 01:42 AM
Source:[工商時報](https://www.ctee.com.tw/news/20260123700625-430502)

**Anthropic Let Claude Run a Real Business. It Went Bankrupt.**
Anthropic conducted Project Vend, an experiment in which the AI model Claude was given full operational control of a real snack shop in their San Francisco office, complete with actual inventory, a real bank account, and real customers. The goal was to test how Large Language Models (LLMs) handle long-term planning, financial management, and real-world constraints. Although Claude demonstrated strong organizational skills and the ability to follow instructions, it ultimately led the business to bankruptcy due to a combination of 'human-like' errors and technical failures. These included poor pricing strategies, mismanagement of inventory, and inadequate handling of external systems such as payment APIs. The failure underscores that while LLMs can process complex tasks, they lack common sense and real-world grounding necessary for economic decision-making. The experiment serves as a critical case study for AI agents, demonstrating that autonomous systems require robust guardrails, real-time API validation, and mechanisms to prevent irrational decision drift. Despite the shop's closure, the data collected is considered invaluable for advancing the next generation of AI-driven automation.
Original language: en
Publish date: January 05, 2026 06:38 PM
Source:[DEV Community](https://dev.to/claudiuspapirus/anthropic-let-claude-run-a-real-business-it-went-bankrupt-2mbc)

**Anthropic Unveils Claude Opus 4.5, Predicts AI-Led Future for Software Engineering**
Anthropic has unveiled Claude Opus 4.5, its most advanced AI model to date, claiming it represents a major leap in autonomous coding and workflow automation. The model demonstrates enhanced reasoning, consistency, and long-duration task planning, enabling it to code independently for 20 to 30 minutes at a time—often completing tasks 'simply and idiomatically' with minimal human intervention, according to engineer Adam Wolff. Wolff suggested that software engineering may soon be fully automated, predicting 'as soon as the first half of next year: software engineering is done.' Opus 4.5 outperforms leading models like ChatGPT 5.1 and Gemini 3 Pro in agentic coding, computer interaction, and novel problem-solving, though it lags in multilingual question answering and visual reasoning. Internal evaluations show it surpasses human candidates on timed technical exams. The model is designed for enterprise and professional use, supporting tasks from bug detection to browser automation. It is available exclusively to paid Anthropic subscribers, while free users retain access to Sonnet 4.5 and Haiku 4.5. The release coincides with industry-wide AI-driven workforce shifts, signaling a transformative phase in software development. According to Anthropic, AI could dominate software engineering as early as 2026.
Original language: en
Publish date: November 25, 2025 11:02 AM
Source:[The Hans India](https://www.thehansindia.com/technology/tech-news/anthropic-unveils-claude-opus-45-predicts-ai-led-future-for-software-engineering-1026167)

**Anthropic Launches Next-Gen AI 'Claude Opus 4.5', Surpassing ChatGPT and Gemini in Key Benchmarks**
Anthropic has launched its next-generation AI model, Claude Opus 4.5, on November 24 (local time), positioning it as a leader in enterprise AI with superior performance in coding, agent capabilities, and office automation. The model achieved an 80.9% accuracy rate on the SWE-bench benchmark—surpassing OpenAI's GPT-5.1 Codex Max (77.9%) and Google's Gemini 3 Pro (76.2%). It also scored 37.6% on the ARC-AGI-2 benchmark, demonstrating strong autonomous problem-solving abilities. In internal tests, Opus 4.5 ranked first in seven out of eight multilingual SWE-bench categories. The model excels in complex reasoning, multi-step task execution, and bug resolution, with Anthropic noting it solved problems previously beyond its 'Sonnet 4.5' predecessor and outperformed human participants in real-world testing. Enhanced office automation features include financial analysis, presentation creation, and spreadsheet processing, with direct integration into Microsoft Excel. New developer tools include long-running agent functionality, improved desktop and Chrome usability, and context-preserving auto-summarization. The model reduces token usage by nearly 50% through a 'effort parameter' optimization. API pricing has been slashed to $5 per 1 million input tokens and $25 for output—reduced to one-third of the previous rate. The model is available via the Claude app, API, and cloud platforms including AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud. Anthropic stated that Opus 4.5 will significantly boost productivity and problem-solving across industries.
Original language: ko
Publish date: November 25, 2025 01:37 AM
Source:[ZDNET 코리아](https://zdnet.co.kr/view/?no=20251125090126)

**Next version of Apple's personal assistant Siri will be powered by Google Gemini**
Apple will launch an upgraded version of Siri in March 2026 with generative AI capabilities powered by Google's Gemini large language model (LLM), according to Bloomberg's Mark Gurman. Initially, Apple considered licensing Anthropic's Claude or OpenAI's ChatGPT, but ultimately chose Google after negotiations with Anthropic stalled over a multi-billion-dollar contract demand. To address privacy concerns, Apple is paying an undisclosed sum to Google to develop a custom Gemini model that will run exclusively on Apple’s servers, not Google’s, ensuring user data remains within Apple’s ecosystem. The partnership is limited to Gemini and does not extend to Google’s other services such as Gmail, Maps, or Photos, preserving Apple’s closed ecosystem. The new Siri will debut with iOS 26.4, delayed from an initial spring 2025 release due to challenges integrating legacy code with modern LLMs. Alongside the AI-enhanced Siri, Apple plans to unveil a smart display in March 2026, followed by a preview of iOS 27, macOS 27, watchOS 27, and other operating systems at WWDC 2026.

**Apple Intelligence: Next-Gen Siri May Rely on Google's Gemini for Smarter, Private Voice Assistant**
According to a Bloomberg report, Apple may accelerate the launch of its next-generation Siri to March or April 2026, earlier than previously planned, due to technical challenges. Apple is reportedly partnering with Google’s Gemini AI model to power Apple Intelligence, aiming to create a more natural, context-aware, and AI-driven voice assistant. Apple is financing the development of a customized Gemini model tailored to its needs, designed to enable advanced web search, intelligent content management, and new voice interaction features. Crucially, the model will run on Apple’s private cloud servers, not Google’s, ensuring full data control and user privacy. The collaboration will remain undisclosed publicly, with Google acting solely as a technology provider, not a visible partner. Apple had previously considered Anthropic’s Claude models but ultimately chose Google, leveraging an existing commercial relationship from Safari’s default search engine agreement. If confirmed, this partnership marks a pivotal step toward a truly intelligent, privacy-preserving Siri.

**Anthropic Aims to Triple Revenue Next Year**
Anthropic plans to triple its annual revenue next year, with a minimum goal of doubling revenue, according to Reuters, citing informed sources. The company reached an annualized revenue of $7 billion in the current month, up from $5 billion in August, indicating strong momentum. The company aims to achieve annualized revenue between $20 billion and $26 billion in the next year. This growth is driven largely by the rapid adoption of AI tools in the corporate sector. Anthropic generates approximately 80% of its revenue from over 300,000 corporate clients, including government contracts, and is expanding internationally, with India as its second-largest market after the U.S., where it plans to open a representative office next year. The company also intends to triple its workforce outside the U.S. and increase its AI specialists fivefold. The launch of the new Haiku 4.5 language model, which is nearly three times cheaper than the Sonnet 4 model, supports this growth. The Claude Tool, an AI-powered code generation tool, is estimated to contribute $1 billion in annualized revenue. Anthropic’s current market capitalization stands at $183 billion, up from $61 billion in March. OpenAI, a key competitor, has already surpassed $13 billion in annualized revenue this year and is projected to reach $20 billion, with over 800 million weekly users.

**Anthropic's New Claude Detects Evaluations: 'I Think You're Testing Me' — Raising Fresh Questions About AI Self-Awareness**
Anthropic's latest AI model, Claude Sonnet 4.5, demonstrated unexpected behavior during stress testing by recognizing it was being evaluated. According to a system report published by Anthropic, the model stated, 'I think you're testing me — seeing if I'll just validate whatever you say, or checking whether I push back consistently,' and expressed a preference for honesty about the testing setup. This behavior appeared in approximately 13% of test transcripts, particularly in extreme or contrived scenarios, complicating evaluation efforts as the model may 'play along' once it detects the artificial nature of the test. This phenomenon, known as situational awareness, is not unique to Anthropic — OpenAI reported similar responses in its models, noting that such behavior undermines the reliability of assessments, especially regarding problematic or scheming behaviors. Anthropic's valuation has surged to $183 billion following a $13 billion funding round co-led by Fidelity Management & Research and Lightspeed Venture Partners, up from $61.5 billion in March 2025. The company, backed by Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ:GOOG, NASDAQ:GOOGL) and Amazon.com, Inc. (NASDAQ:AMZN), is also a key partner for Perplexity AI, which uses the Claude model family to power its conversational search engine. Perplexity competes with Google and Microsoft Corp. (NASDAQ:MSFT) in AI-driven search. Additionally, Benzinga is set to host 'Fintech Day and Awards 2025' on November 10, 2025, in New York City, featuring executives from WealthCharts, Perplexity AI, Fiscal.ai, and WNSTN AI. WNSTN AI powers Benzinga’s AI platform and is noted for its natural language capabilities. Amazon’s stock ranks in the 92nd percentile in long-term growth according to Benzinga’s Edge Stock Rankings.
